# Project Status

_Updated by: Lead agent_ &nbsp;·&nbsp; _When: 2026-07-15 (planning pass 6)_

## Phase

**build** — Phase 1–6 (engine + UI scaffold) is fully built and closed out (#4–#30 all CLOSED
except #31/#38 below). Project is now in a **post-MVP feedback/hardening loop**: client is
playing the running simulation and filing bug reports / feature requests directly as issues
(#32–#44, with #32 CLOSED/karen-PASSED), and the team is also fixing its own dispatcher
infrastructure (#46–#55, see below).

## New this pass — #52 karen-FAILED a 2nd time on a self-inflicted trap; broke the loop directly

**#52 attempt 2 result:** karen FAILED on exactly one gap: `state/STATUS.md` and
`state/worker_output.txt` showed as modified/uncommitted alongside the 5 intentional #33 WIP
files. Root cause: `state/STATUS.md` is rewritten by the **lead** every planning pass and
`state/worker_output.txt` is overwritten by **every worker** on every issue — neither file is
ever committed anywhere in the loop (that's the still-open #53), so they perpetually show as
"extra" dirty files no matter how clean a worker leaves everything else. This was an
unwinnable, self-resetting target: every lead pass (including this one) re-dirties STATUS.md
before the next worker attempt even starts.

**Fix applied this pass (breaks the trap, doesn't just retry blind):**
1. Rewrote #52's body (attempt 3) to explicitly exclude `state/STATUS.md` and
   `state/worker_output.txt` from its "clean tree" done-when criterion — those two are declared
   lead/dispatcher housekeeping, not worker deliverables, and their presence as modified files is
   expected and NOT a gap.
2. Added `state/verdict.txt`, `state/tmp_body_*.md`, `state/tmp_issue_*.md`, `.codex/` to
   `.gitignore` — these are the exact scratch-clutter classes #52 has already had to clean out
   twice; ignoring them stops them from ever being flagged as "extra untracked files" again for
   any future issue.
3. As lead, directly committed `.gitignore`, `state/STATUS.md`, and `state/worker_output.txt`
   (housekeeping only — the 5 `#33` WIP files were left untouched/uncommitted, per #33's
   requirements). This gives #52's 3rd attempt a genuinely clean starting tree instead of one
   that fails before the worker even opens a file.

This is a one-time manual workaround; **#53** (dispatcher auto-commit after karen PASS) remains
the real structural fix and is still next in queue after #52.

## Prior pass finding — #55 filed (dispatcher retry doesn't see karen's prior verdict)

**#52 attempt 1 result:** karen FAILED it on 3 narrow gaps — `src/tests/diag.test.ts` scratch
file not deleted, `state/worker_output.txt` not written, and unrelated clutter left in the tree
(16 `state/tmp_body_*.md`/`tmp_issue_*.md` scratch files, `.codex/`, plus a new untracked
`src/tests/monoculture.test.ts` that isn't in #32/#33 scope and currently fails
`npm test` outright — `expected 13021 to be less than 200`). The actual hard part (#32 vs #33
file-level split, #32 commit) was verified correct — karen's PASS items confirm commit
`3dd34098b5d2793fcb9c9e077d1d8bc62e50886d` is clean and isolated.

**Root cause found while re-queuing #52:** `scripts/dispatcher.sh`'s worker prompt is built from
the issue's **original body only** (`iss_body` from `gh issue view ... body`) — it never
includes karen's verdict comment. So a failed attempt's retry starts from zero knowledge of what
was already tried or why it was rejected. This is a **structural gap in the retry loop**, not
specific to #52 — every multi-attempt issue in this project's history has been retrying blind.
Filed **#55** (`agent-backlog`, `depends_on: #52, #54`) to fix this generally in dispatcher.sh.

**Immediate workaround applied this pass:** manually rewrote #52's issue body (still
`agent-todo`, same queue slot — it's the lowest-numbered issue so it dispatches next
automatically) to fold in karen's exact 3 gaps as explicit "Done when" items, plus a "Progress so
far" section confirming the #32 commit is already done so the next attempt doesn't redo it. This
gets #52 unblocked now without waiting on #55.

## Prior finding (superseded by the above where it discusses #52, kept for the starvation-fix history)

Last pass's tactical fix (backlogging #33 behind #52) worked exactly as intended — the
dispatcher moved on and picked up #34 next. But #34 then failed karen (see verdict below) and,
being relabeled back to `agent-todo` with the same low number, became the new "oldest" issue —
**re-starving #52 all over again**. #52 (URGENT — uncommitted work at real risk of loss) has now
gone **9+ full lead passes with zero worker dispatch attempts**, purely because dispatcher.sh
always picks the oldest-numbered `agent-todo` issue (root cause tracked in #54) and something
low-numbered keeps failing karen and reclaiming that slot.

Whack-a-mole single-issue fixes clearly won't keep up. This pass I broke the deadlock directly:
**every currently-open `agent-todo` issue except #52, #53, #54 was moved to `agent-backlog`**
with `depends_on: #52, #54` added to each (#34, #35, #36, #39, #40, #41, #42, #43, #44, #46,
#47, #48, #49, #50 — 14 issues, each has an explanatory comment). This guarantees:
1. The **very next** worker slot goes to **#52** (only remaining low-numbered agent-todo issue) —
   finally unblocks the URGENT git cleanup.
2. After #52 closes, **#53** and then **#54** (the actual dispatcher fairness fix) get the next
   slots — #54 landing is what stops this pattern from recurring.
3. All 14 backlogged issues auto-promote back to `agent-todo` together once **both** #52 and #54
   are closed, at which point the fixed (non-starving) dispatcher takes over normal ordering.

This is a temporary queue-priority measure, not a claim that those 14 issues are blocked on
#52/#54 technically (most aren't) — it's explicit in each comment. Net effect: **only #52, #53,
#54 are `agent-todo` right now**; nothing else will dispatch until #52 and #54 both close.

## Prior-pass finding (still accurate, kept for history)

Moved **#33 to `agent-backlog`, `depends_on: #52`** — genuine technical dependency (not queue
fairness): #33's last two karen FAILs both cited its diff being entangled with #32's uncommitted
files, which #52 exists to fix. Confirmed still correctly labeled `agent-backlog` this pass.

**#34 karen verdict (prior pass, FAILED):** worker added `applyBiodiversityPressure()` (density-
dependent mortality for monoculture/overcrowding) — mechanism exists but a fixed stochastic
penalty can't keep pace with a reproducing monoculture (40→13,021 creatures in 50 ticks, 26x the
configured 500 cap). This is the same `monoculture.test.ts` file #52 is now cleaning out of the
tree (see above) — the real fix for #34 is still queued in `agent-backlog`, unaffected.

The **no `git add`/`git commit` step anywhere in dispatcher.sh** root cause (of the #32/#33/#34
entanglement) is tracked by #52 (one-time cleanup, in progress, attempt 2 next) and #53
(root-cause fix going forward, not yet attempted).

## What this project is

Project Origins: a persistent ecosystem simulation where players guide life evolution through
species creation and environmental stewardship. MVP is a deterministic, local-first evolution
sandbox validating player attachment and emergent adaptation.

## Client decisions (all answered — #18 CLOSED)

| Q | Decision |
|---|----------|
| Q1 State mgmt | **Zustand** + separate event log for lineage/replay |
| Q2 Visualization | **Canvas 2D** for MVP |
| Q3 Energy budget | Radial solar dissipation from grid center (peak=10 at center) + **God Mode** control panel |
| Q4 First-play world | Pre-built barely-sustainable ecosystem spanning full ecological spectrum |

- **#52** — karen re-ran twice: attempt 1 failed on 3 gaps (scratch file, missing output file,

## Blockers

- **Dispatcher infra outage (2026-07-14 17:00 – 2026-07-15 02:00, #45)**: ~9.5hr near-total rc=1
  outage across worker/karen roles (lead partially functional). **Confirmed resolved** — 5+
  consecutive clean passes since 02:00. #45 closed. Root cause (OAuth expiry vs rate-limit vs
  credential-path difference) was never confirmed by the client and doesn't need to be — pattern
  fully recovered on its own.
- **Attempt-counter bug (#48, still open)**: `attempt_count` in dispatcher.sh counts worker-fail
  comments across an issue's *entire history*, never resetting on requeue. This caused #22, #23,
  #26, #31, #32, #33 to hit false `agent-blocked` states purely from the infra outage above, with
  zero real content-attempt failures. **This pass:** found `max_worker_attempts` had reverted to
  the default of 3 in `schedule.json` (an earlier stopgap bump to 10 never actually landed) — a
  requeue at exactly 3 lifetime attempts insta-blocks again with zero new dispatch under that
  default. Re-applied `max_worker_attempts: 10` in `schedule.json` (committed directly to main,
  operational/infra config) and manually relabeled **#32** `agent-blocked` → `agent-todo` with a
  comment explaining the requeue. Will revert the 3→10 bump once #48's proper self-resetting fix
  lands and closes.
